title: St Benedict's Church, Paddlesworth

infobox.value: is a [redundant](/wiki/Redundant_church "Redundant church") [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") church in the village of Paddlesworth, [Kent](/wiki/Kent "Kent"), England. It is recorded in the [National Heritage List for England](/wiki/National_Heritage_List_for_England "National Heritage List for England") as a designated Grade II\* [listed building](/wiki/Listed_building#England_and_Wales "Listed building"),[[1]](#cite_note-hg-1) and is under the care of the [Churches Conservation Trust](/wiki/Churches_Conservation_Trust "Churches Conservation Trust").[[2]](#cite_note-cct-2) The church stands on the [Pilgrims' Way](/wiki/Pilgrims%27_Way "Pilgrims' Way"), about 1 mile (1.6 km) west of [Snodland](/wiki/Snodland "Snodland").[[2]](#cite_note-cct-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[*Paddlesworth, St Benedict's Church*](https://web.archive.org/web/20100427004744/http://www.britainexpress.com/attractions.htm?attraction=4045), Britain Express, archived from [the original](http://www.britainexpress.com/attractions.htm?attraction=4045) on 27 April 2010, retrieved 28 March 2011

**St Benedict's Church** is a [redundant](/wiki/Redundant_church "Redundant church") [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") church in the village of Paddlesworth, [Kent](/wiki/Kent "Kent"), England. It is recorded in the [National Heritage List for England](/wiki/National_Heritage_List_for_England "National Heritage List for England") as a designated Grade II\* [listed building](/wiki/Listed_building#England_and_Wales "Listed building"),[[1]](#cite_note-hg-1) and is under the care of the [Churches Conservation Trust](/wiki/Churches_Conservation_Trust "Churches Conservation Trust").[[2]](#cite_note-cct-2) The church stands on the [Pilgrims' Way](/wiki/Pilgrims%27_Way "Pilgrims' Way"), about 1 mile (1.6 km) west of [Snodland](/wiki/Snodland "Snodland").[[2]](#cite_note-cct-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St_Benedict%27s_Church,_Paddlesworth&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The church dates from the early part of the 12th century, and was modified during the following century.[[1]](#cite_note-hg-1) It closed as a church in 1678, and was then used for some 250 years for non-religious purposes.[[2]](#cite_note-cct-2) It is located by a farm, and was used as a farm building.[[2]](#cite_note-cct-2)[[4]](#cite_note-4) The church was restored in the early 20th century, and again later in the century.[[1]](#cite_note-hg-1)

## Architecture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St_Benedict%27s_Church,_Paddlesworth&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Architecture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/34/St_Benedict%27s_Church%2C_Paddlesworth%2C_Snodland.jpg/250px-St_Benedict%27s_Church%2C_Paddlesworth%2C_Snodland.jpg)](/wiki/File:St_Benedict%27s_Church,_Paddlesworth,_Snodland.jpg)

View of the church from the northeastern corner

St Benedict's is constructed in [ragstone](/wiki/Rag-stone "Rag-stone") [rubble](/wiki/Rubble "Rubble"), and has a tiled roof. Its plan is simple, consisting of two cells, a [nave](/wiki/Nave "Nave") and a [chancel](/wiki/Chancel "Chancel"). The windows and doors are round-headed. The interior is plain, and it contains a gallery added in the 19th century. Also in the nave is a [cast iron](/wiki/Cast_iron "Cast iron") [candelabrum](/wiki/Candelabrum "Candelabrum") dating from the late 17th century.[[1]](#cite_note-hg-1)
